questions.question: Explain why the courtiers called the prince the Happy Prince.
questions.answer: The courtiers called him the Happy Prince because he lived a life of luxury in a palace where sorrow was not allowed to enter. He enjoyed pleasures and did not know about the suffering of the poor people in the city.

questions.question: Describe the role of the swallow in the story.
questions.answer: The swallow plays an important role as the helper and messenger of the Happy Prince. He carries the jewels and gold of the prince to the poor people of the city. Through his loyalty and sacrifice, he helps the prince bring happiness to many people.

questions.question: Why did the prince ask the swallow to take the ruby to the seamstress?
questions.answer: The prince saw a poor seamstress whose child was suffering from fever. She had no money to buy medicine or food. Therefore, he asked the swallow to take the ruby from his sword and give it to her so she could sell it and help her child.

questions.question: How did the prince help the young playwright?
questions.answer: The prince saw a young playwright who was hungry and cold while trying to finish his play. The prince asked the swallow to pluck out one of his sapphire eyes and give it to the playwright so he could sell it and buy food and firewood.

questions.question: Explain how the prince helped the match girl.
questions.answer: The prince saw a little match girl who had dropped her matches into the gutter and was afraid to go home without money. The prince asked the swallow to give his second sapphire eye to the girl so she could sell it and avoid punishment.

questions.question: Why did the swallow decide to stay with the prince?
questions.answer: The swallow decided to stay with the prince because he felt sympathy for the prince and admired his kindness. He also developed a deep friendship with the prince and wanted to help him continue helping the poor.

questions.question: Describe how the prince helped the poor people in the city.
questions.answer: The prince asked the swallow to remove the gold leaves from his body and distribute them to the poor people. The gold helped them buy food and survive the harsh conditions.

questions.question: What did the swallow observe when he flew over the city?
questions.answer: The swallow observed the contrast between rich and poor people. Rich people lived happily in beautiful houses while poor people suffered from hunger and cold.

questions.question: Explain the sacrifice made by the Happy Prince.
questions.answer: The Happy Prince sacrificed all his jewels and gold to help the poor. He gave away his ruby, both sapphire eyes, and all the gold leaves on his body so that the needy people could live better lives.

questions.question: Describe the swallow’s final moments.
questions.answer: The swallow became weak due to the cold winter. Before dying, he flew up to the prince, kissed him on the lips, and then fell dead at the prince’s feet.

questions.question: What happened after the swallow died?
questions.answer: After the swallow died, the prince’s lead heart broke into two pieces. This showed the deep bond between the prince and the swallow.

questions.question: Why did the Mayor decide to remove the statue?
questions.answer: The Mayor believed the statue had become ugly and useless because it had lost its gold and jewels. Therefore he ordered it to be pulled down.

questions.question: What happened to the statue after it was removed?
questions.answer: The statue was melted in a furnace. However, the lead heart did not melt and was thrown on a dust heap along with the dead swallow.

questions.question: Why could the prince’s heart not melt in the furnace?
questions.answer: The lead heart could not melt because it was very hard and symbolised the prince’s strong love and compassion.

questions.question: Why were the prince and swallow considered precious by God?
questions.answer: God considered them precious because they showed kindness, compassion, and selfless sacrifice while helping the poor people.

questions.question: Explain the friendship between the prince and the swallow.
questions.answer: The prince and the swallow develop a deep friendship through their acts of kindness. The swallow becomes the prince’s helper and remains loyal to him until death.

questions.question: What moral lesson does the story teach?
questions.answer: The story teaches that kindness, compassion, and sacrifice are more valuable than wealth and beauty.

questions.question: How does the prince realise the suffering of people?
questions.answer: From his high position as a statue, the prince observes the misery, poverty, and suffering of the people in the city.

questions.question: Why is the swallow considered a loyal friend?
questions.answer: The swallow is considered loyal because he stays with the prince and helps him even during winter, despite knowing it may cost his life.

questions.question: Explain how the story highlights social inequality.
questions.answer: The story shows the contrast between rich people living comfortably and poor people suffering from hunger and poverty in the city.

questions.question: What transformation happens to the prince after death?
questions.answer: After death, the prince becomes aware of human suffering and devotes himself to helping the poor.

questions.question: What role does sacrifice play in the story?
questions.answer: Sacrifice plays a major role because both the prince and the swallow give up their comfort and life to help others.

questions.question: How does the story define true happiness?
questions.answer: The story shows that true happiness comes from helping others and showing kindness rather than living in luxury.

questions.question: Explain the significance of the story’s ending.
questions.answer: The ending highlights that kindness and sacrifice are the most valuable qualities. God rewards the prince and the swallow for their goodness.

questions.question: Summarise the story 'The Happy Prince'.
questions.answer: The story describes how the statue of the Happy Prince and a swallow help the poor people of a city. The prince sacrifices his jewels and gold, and the swallow sacrifices his life. Their kindness is finally recognised by God.
